qa.question: What does "Whenever an opponent is selecting cards for an ability, if they can select this card, they must select it" mean?
qa.answer: It means that when an opponent is playing an ability that requires them to select one or more cards, such as "Select an enemy follower on the field and deal it 2 damage" (found on Angelic Snipe, Bellringer Angel's evolved form, etc.), if they are able to select this follower, then they must select it.Note that this ability is only valid when it's on the field. Also, when an opponent is attacking with a follower, they don't have to select this follower as the attack target.

qa.question: Can you explain "Whenever an opponent is selecting cards for an ability, if they can select this card, they must select it" in detail?
qa.answer: ccording to the Comprehensive Rules, "if multiple effects that require a player to perform a certain action are produced simultaneously, and it is not possible to perform all of them, the player is required to select and perform as many of the actions as possible."Let's look at some examples of this in action. In the following, X denotes a follower with "Whenever an opponent is selecting cards for an ability, if they can select this card, they must select it," and Y denotes a follower that doesn't have this ability.Example 1: Your field has 1 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 1" follower for an ability. In this case, they must select the X.Example 2: Your field has 2 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 1" follower for an ability. In this case, they must select one of the two Xs.Example 3: Your field has 1 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 2" followers for an ability. In this case, they must select the X, but whether or not they select a Y is up to them.Example 4: Your field has 2 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 2" followers for an ability. In this case, they must select both Xs.Example 5: Your field has 3 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 2" followers for an ability. In this case, they must select two of the three Xs.

qa.question: What does "steal" mean?
qa.answer: If you "steal" an enemy follower on the field, it means you move it to your field and become its controller, allowing you to use it and its abilities. When a stolen follower moves to a zone other than the field, it moves to the corresponding zone of its owner.

qa.question: Can I steal an enemy follower token on the field?

If my field is full, can I still steal an enemy follower?
qa.answer: No, but you can still play this follower's activated ability. In that case, you would select an enemy follower on the field, pay the cost by banishing 3 Fallen Angel cards in your cemetery, then, as your field is full, simply refresh the selected follower without moving it to your field.

qa.question: If I steal a follower with an evolve/serve ability, can I evolve/race it?

qa.question: If I steal a follower, can I attack with it in the same turn?

qa.question: If I steal a follower with Storm or Rush, can I attack with it in the same turn?

qa.answer: Not unless it has Rush or Storm, and evolving a follower doesn't technically give it either. The rule that allows evolved followers to attack engaged enemy followers immediately only applies on the turn it evolves.

qa.question: If I steal a racing Haru Urara from an opponent, can I immediately attack engaged enemy followers with it?
qa.answer: Yes. Racing followers have Rush, and followers don't lose Rush when moving from one player's field to another.

qa.answer: You pay the cost by giving -3 defense to your leader. Then, the Mordecai the Duelist is put onto its owner's field.When destroyed, Mordecai the Duelist moves from your field to its owner's cemetery. This triggers its Last Words ability, which you choose to play by paying the cost. After paying the cost, you play the ability, but by this time, Mordecai the Duelist has moved to its owner's cemetery, so it's put onto its owner's field, not yours.

qa.question: If a Dark Alice I stole from an opponent is destroyed, and I play its Last Words ability, what happens?
qa.answer: You pay the cost by banishing the top 10 cards of your deck. Then, the Dark Alice is put onto its owner's field, and you discard a card.When destroyed, the Dark Alice moves from your field to its owner's cemetery. This triggers its Last Words ability, which you choose to play by paying the cost. After paying the cost, you play the ability, but by this time, the Dark Alice has moved to its owner's cemetery, so it's put onto its owner's field, not yours. Lastly, since you're the one playing the Last Words ability, you discard a card.

What happens if a follower with the Follower Strike ability from this amulet attacks an enemy follower with 2 defense?
qa.answer: The attacking follower remains on the field in an engaged state, while the enemy follower is destroyed.First, you select the enemy follower as the attack target and engage the attacking follower. Then, during the following Confirmation Timing, you resolve the attacker's Follower Strike ability, dealing 2 damage to the enemy follower and reducing its defense to 0. The enemy follower is then destroyed. As there is no longer an attack target, the attack ends without the followers entering combat, and the attacking follower simply remains in an engaged state.

qa.answer: It means that when an opponent is playing an ability that requires them to select one or more cards, such as "Select an enemy follower on the field and deal it 2 damage" (found on Angelic Snipe, Bellringer Angel's evolved form, etc.), if they are able to select this follower, then they must select it.Note that this ability is only valid when it's on the field. Also, when an opponent is attacking with a follower, they don't have to select this follower as the attack target.

qa.question: Can you explain "Whenever an opponent is selecting cards for an ability, if they can select this card, they must select it" in detail?
qa.answer: ccording to the Comprehensive Rules, "if multiple effects that require a player to perform a certain action are produced simultaneously, and it is not possible to perform all of them, the player is required to select and perform as many of the actions as possible."Let's look at some examples of this in action. In the following, X denotes a follower with "Whenever an opponent is selecting cards for an ability, if they can select this card, they must select it," and Y denotes a follower that doesn't have this ability.Example 1: Your field has 1 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 1" follower for an ability. In this case, they must select the X.Example 2: Your field has 2 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 1" follower for an ability. In this case, they must select one of the two Xs.Example 3: Your field has 1 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 2" followers for an ability. In this case, they must select the X, but whether or not they select a Y is up to them.Example 4: Your field has 2 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 2" followers for an ability. In this case, they must select both Xs.Example 5: Your field has 3 X and 2 Y. An opponent is selecting "up to 2" followers for an ability. In this case, they must select two of the three Xs.

qa.question: If I steal a follower with Storm or Rush, can I attack with it in the same turn?
If I steal a follower with "This follower can't attack enemies," can I attack its owner's leader with it?
If I steal an evolved follower, can I immediately attack engaged enemy followers with it?
qa.answer: Not unless it has Rush or Storm, and evolving a follower doesn't technically give it either. The rule that allows evolved followers to attack engaged enemy followers immediately only applies on the turn it evolves.

qa.question: If I steal a racing Haru Urara from an opponent, can I immediately attack engaged enemy followers with it?
qa.answer: Yes. Racing followers have Rush, and followers don't lose Rush when moving from one player's field to another.

qa.question: If a follower I stole from an opponent is destroyed, where does it go?
If a follower I stole from an opponent is banished, where does it go?
If a follower I stole from an opponent is returned to hand, whose hand does it return to?
If a follower I stole from an opponent is put into the EX area, whose EX area does it move to?
If an evolved or racing follower I stole from an opponent leaves the field, where do I put its evolved form or Carrot(s)?
qa.answer: Faceup in its owner's evolve deck.

qa.question: If a follower I stole from an opponent is destroyed, who plays its Last Words ability?
If an Aiela, Dragon Knight I stole from an opponent is destroyed, and I play its Last Words ability, what happens?
qa.answer: You increase your maximum play points by 1.

qa.question: If an evolved Leonidas I stole from an opponent is destroyed, and I play its Last Words ability, what happens?
qa.answer: You summon a Leonidas's Resolve token onto your field.

qa.question: If an Underworld Watchman Khawy I stole from an opponent is destroyed, and I play its Last Words ability, what happens?
qa.answer: You select an enemy follower on the field, destroy it, and give your leader +X defense.

qa.question: If a Mordecai the Duelist I stole from an opponent is destroyed, and I play its Last Words ability, what happens?
qa.answer: You pay the cost by giving -3 defense to your leader. Then, the Mordecai the Duelist is put onto its owner's field.When destroyed, Mordecai the Duelist moves from your field to its owner's cemetery. This triggers its Last Words ability, which you choose to play by paying the cost. After paying the cost, you play the ability, but by this time, Mordecai the Duelist has moved to its owner's cemetery, so it's put onto its owner's field, not yours.

qa.question: If a Dark Alice I stole from an opponent is destroyed, and I play its Last Words ability, what happens?
qa.answer: You pay the cost by banishing the top 10 cards of your deck. Then, the Dark Alice is put onto its owner's field, and you discard a card.When destroyed, the Dark Alice moves from your field to its owner's cemetery. This triggers its Last Words ability, which you choose to play by paying the cost. After paying the cost, you play the ability, but by this time, the Dark Alice has moved to its owner's cemetery, so it's put onto its owner's field, not yours. Lastly, since you're the one playing the Last Words ability, you discard a card.

qa.question: If I steal an enemy Slade, Blossoming Wolf and then it's returned to hand from my field, what happens?
qa.answer: The Slade, Blossoming Wolf returns to its owner's hand, and you play its "When this card is returned to hand from your field" ability.

qa.question: If I steal an enemy Lance Lizard with this follower's ability, does Lance Lizard's "Once on each of your turns, when this follower is selected for an ability" trigger?
qa.answer: No. When the Lance Lizard is selected, it's not its controller's turn.

qa.question: If a card or ability cost requires me to put a follower from my field into the cemetery, can I use a stolen follower to pay it?
qa.answer: Yes. In that case, you would put the stolen follower into its owner's cemetery, not your own.

qa.question: If I have 2 copies of this follower on my field, can I activate both their abilities?
If a follower I steal says to summon a token, but I don't have that token, what do I do?
qa.answer: Use some other object that all players can distinguish as that token.
